# CLAUDE.md
<!-- Auto-generated by Copilot Agent | Repo: infinity-heroes-bedtime-chronicles -->

## Project Overview

Infinity Heroes: Bedtime Chronicles is an AI-powered bedtime story Progressive Web App designed for children ages 7–9. Parents or kids choose a hero name, superpower, and setting, then Google Gemini generates a personalized multi-chapter story with illustrations and optional TTS narration. The app is deployed on Vercel and targets COPPA compliance: no analytics, no PII collection, no external links.

## Stack

- **Language**: TypeScript 5.8 (strict, `noEmit`)
- **Framework**: React 19 with functional components and hooks
- **Runtime**: Browser (SPA) + Vercel Serverless Functions (Node 20)
- **Build tool**: Vite 6 with `@tailwindcss/vite` and `vite-plugin-pwa`
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4
- **Animations**: Framer Motion 11
- **AI**: Google Gemini via `@google/genai` — text, image, and TTS generation
- **Storage**: IndexedDB (`StorageManager`) for stories, audio cache, and preferences
- **PWA**: Workbox auto-update, offline support
- **Testing**: Vitest 4 + React Testing Library 16 + happy-dom + fake-indexeddb
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions (type-check → lint → test → build → security audit → license scan) + Vercel auto-deploy

## Architecture

```
Browser (SPA)
  └── React UI (App.tsx)
        ├── useStoryEngine (state machine, orchestration)
        ├── AIClient (fetch → /api/* serverless functions)
        ├── NarrationManager (Web Audio, PCM 24 kHz decode)
        ├── SoundManager (procedural ambient noise via Web Audio API)
        └── StorageManager (IndexedDB: stories, audio, preferences)

Vercel Serverless Functions (/api/*.ts)
  ├── /api/generate-story      → Gemini text (gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20)
  ├── /api/generate-avatar     → Gemini image (gemini-2.0-flash-preview-image-generation)
  ├── /api/generate-scene      → Gemini image (gemini-2.0-flash-preview-image-generation)
  └── /api/generate-narration  → Gemini TTS (gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts)

Google Gemini API
  └── Reached only by serverless functions; GEMINI_API_KEY never leaves the server
```

No background jobs, queues, cron, or webhooks.

## Key Directories

```
/                       Root — source files live at top level
  App.tsx               Root React component; lazy-loads Setup and ReadingView
  Book.tsx              Story book / page-layout component
  Setup.tsx             Setup-phase orchestrator
  Panel.tsx             Comic-panel renderer
  HeroHeader.tsx        Hero avatar header display
  LoadingFX.tsx         Loading animation
  ApiKeyDialog.tsx      API key input dialog (parent/guardian facing)
  AIClient.ts           Client-side API abstraction with retry logic
  NarrationManager.ts   Web Audio TTS playback engine (PCM decoding)
  SoundManager.ts       Procedural ambient sound synthesizer
  useApiKey.ts          API key validation hook
  types.ts              All shared TypeScript types
  index.tsx             React entry point
  index.html            HTML shell

  api/                  Vercel serverless functions
    _aiClient.ts        Shared GoogleGenAI client factory
    _middleware.ts      withMiddleware wrapper, input sanitisers, rate-limit
    generate-story.ts   Story text generation endpoint
    generate-avatar.ts  Hero avatar image endpoint
    generate-scene.ts   Scene illustration endpoint
    generate-narration.ts TTS audio endpoint

  components/           Reusable UI components
    setup/              Setup-phase sub-components (ClassicSetup, MadlibsSetup, …)
    ReadingView.tsx     Main story reading experience
    CompletionView.tsx  End-of-story summary (badge, vocab, joke)
    SyncedText.tsx      Narration-synced text highlighting
    SettingsModal.tsx   User preferences modal
    ErrorBoundary.tsx   React error boundary

  hooks/
    useStoryEngine.ts   Core story state machine & orchestration
    useNarrationSync.ts AudioContext-to-React state bridge

  lib/
    StorageManager.ts   IndexedDB persistence (stories, audio, preferences)
    Logger.ts           Structured console logger (use instead of console.*)
    ErrorHandler.ts     classifyApiError() — shared API error classification

  test/
    setup.ts            Vitest global test setup (RTL + jest-dom)

  docs/                 Comprehensive project documentation (20+ files)
  scripts/              Build/utility scripts (generate-icons.mjs)
  public/               Static assets (icons, sounds, privacy policy)
```

## Environment Variables

Server-side only (Vercel project settings — never commit values):

| Variable | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | Yes | Google Gemini API key used by all `/api/*` serverless functions. |
| `NODE_ENV` | Auto | Set by Vercel/Node; used by `_middleware.ts` to skip key validation in dev/test. |

Client code must never access `GEMINI_API_KEY`. There are no `import.meta.env.*` references in the codebase.

## Development Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start dev server (port 3000)
npm run dev

# Type-check (no emit)
npm run typecheck

# Lint
npm run lint

# Lint with auto-fix
npm run lint:fix

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test

# Run tests once (CI)
npm run test:run

# Run tests with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Production build
npm run build

# Preview production build
npm run preview
```

## Conventions

- **File naming**: PascalCase for React components and utility classes (`App.tsx`, `StorageManager.ts`); camelCase for hooks (`useStoryEngine.ts`); kebab-case for API routes (`generate-story.ts`)
- **Component structure**: Functional components with `const MyComponent: React.FC = () => {}` pattern; props interfaces defined above the component
- **Path aliases**: Use `@/` to reference files from the project root (configured in `tsconfig.json` and `vite.config.ts`)
- **Styling**: Tailwind v4 utility classes inline in JSX — no separate CSS modules
- **Animations**: Framer Motion `motion.*` components and `AnimatePresence` for enter/exit
- **Logging**: Use `logger` from `lib/Logger.ts` in all client code. `console.*` is acceptable only in `api/*.ts` serverless functions
- **Error handling**: try/catch with `logger.error()` and context message; use `classifyApiError()` from `lib/ErrorHandler.ts` for Gemini API errors
- **Async pattern**: `async/await` throughout — no raw Promise chains or callbacks
- **Imports**: React → third-party → components → hooks → utilities → types

## Security Notes

- **Auth model**: None. The Gemini API key is the only credential; it is held server-side in Vercel env vars and never sent to the browser.
- **Secrets**: All via environment variables only — never commit credentials or API keys.
- **Child safety (COPPA)**: No analytics, no PII collection, no external links, no ads, no social media integration, no third-party cookies. Hero name stored only in local React state (never sent to any server except as part of the story prompt).
- **CSP**: Enforced via `vercel.json` — restricts scripts, allows connections only to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`.
- **Input validation**: All API route inputs sanitised with `validateString()` / `validateStringEnum()` from `_middleware.ts` before reaching Gemini.
- **ESLint rules**: `no-eval`, `no-new-func`, `no-script-url` are hard errors to prevent injection.

## Known Issues / TODOs

- `docs/development/CODE_QUALITY.md:412` — `# TODO: Add linting and tests` (outdated; linting and tests are already implemented in CI — this doc comment can be removed).
- `App.tsx:93` — SettingsModal reset callback uses an inline spread instead of a constant for default preferences.
- `components/ReadingView.tsx` — `onGenerateSceneIndex` and `onSubmitFeedback` props are declared in the interface and passed from `App.tsx` but not called inside the component body (reserved for future use).

## Agent Instructions (for Copilot / Claude Code)

- Always run `npm run typecheck && npm run lint` before marking a task complete.
- Always run `npm run test:run` before marking a task complete.
- Do NOT modify `LICENSE`, `vercel.json` (CSP headers), `api/_middleware.ts` (security layer), or `docs/compliance/`.
- Match patterns from `hooks/useStoryEngine.ts` (state management), `api/_middleware.ts` (API route structure), and `lib/StorageManager.ts` (IndexedDB patterns).
- New npm dependencies require explicit user approval.
- All secrets via environment variables only — never hardcode API keys.
- All new `.ts` / `.tsx` files must begin with the Apache-2.0 SPDX license header:
  ```
  /**
   * @license
   * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
   */
  ```
- CI verifies license headers automatically; the build will fail if any `.ts`/`.tsx` file is missing the header.
